content.claim: Cross-national evidence from 2000-2021 meta-analysis shows tracking and school segregation are associated with negative effects on inequality of academic performance, with no significant documented efficiency losses from de-tracking
content.context: Fresh live research through the Demography lens
limitations: Meta-analysis aggregates systems with different institutional contexts; Hungary-specific effects not isolated
Efficiency outcome null result does not prove de-tracking is cost-neutral for school management or capacity utilization
Does not establish what efficiency or equity outcomes Hungary would observe under specific counterfactual policies
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
